iterations.policy:
1) Information Gathering Rules
- IF a user requests any account/order action (cancel/return/exchange/modify) → THEN ask the user to provide either (a) first name, last name, and zip code OR (b) an email address. (Observed in all conversations.)
- IF the user provides first name, last name, and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip). (Conversations 0, 113, 15.)
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) immediately. (Conversations 0, 113, 15.)
- IF the user provides an email address → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email). (Conversation 11.)
- IF find_user_id_by_email returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) immediately. (Conversation 11.)
- IF get_user_details returns a user record containing an orders list → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order_id listed in that user record. (Conversations 0, 113, 11, 15.)
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): IF the agent mistypes a tool name (example: ind_user_id_by_email) → THEN retry with find_user_id_by_email. (Single instance in Conversation 11.)

2) Selection Rules
- IF the user asks to act on orders with a specified status (e.g., "cancel all my pending orders") → THEN filter the user’s orders by order.status equal to the requested status and report back which orders were found and ask whether to proceed on those filtered orders. (Conversation 113.)
- IF the user requests an action on a particular order’s items (exchange/return/modify) → THEN verify the items are present in that order by examining the order details returned by get_order_details before proposing or performing any action. (Conversations 0, 11, 15.)
- IF the user requests a change to product options (e.g., switch type, compatibility, size) → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) for that product to enumerate variants and availability/prices before selecting a replacement item. (Conversations 0, 1, 15.)
- IF the exact variant matching all of the user’s explicitly requested attributes does not exist (availability==false or no variant matches) → THEN present alternative trade-offs derived from the available variants (examples: “full size but different backlight” OR “RGB but different size”) and ask the user which trade-off they prefer. (Conversation 0.)
- IF the user states attribute priorities (e.g., "full size and clicky are my priorities") → THEN select the variant that matches the user’s highest-priority attributes even if other non-prioritized attributes differ. (Conversation 0: selected full-size + clicky even though backlight differed.)

3) Action Execution Rules
- IF a user confirms the requested change after the assistant’s confirmation prompt → THEN perform the matched tool action appropriate to the order state:
  - IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requested an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). (Conversation 0.)
  - IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requested to return delivered items → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id). (Conversation 11.)
  - IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requested to modify pending items (size/variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). (Conversation 15.)
  - IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requested cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). (Conversation 113.)
- WHEN invoking exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, or modify_pending_order_items → THEN pass payment_method_id equal to the original payment method used for that order (the payment_method_id found in the order’s payment_history). (Conversations 0, 11, 15.)
- WHEN invoking exchange_delivered_order_items, modify_pending_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items → THEN pass item_ids that correspond exactly to the original item_ids from the order and new_item_ids that correspond exactly to the item_id(s) of the chosen variant(s) from get_product_details. (Conversations 0, 15, 11.)
- IF cancel_pending_order is invoked → THEN include the cancellation reason provided by the user as the reason argument. (Conversation 113.)

4) Confirmation Rules
- IF the assistant is about to execute an exchange, modify, return, or cancel action → THEN ask the user for explicit confirmation of the requested change (examples used: "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?" or "Ok are you sure?") before calling the tool. (Conversations 0, 11, 15.)
- IF the user has not specified required variant details (e.g., backlight color or thermostat color) → THEN ask for those missing variant choices before requesting confirmation and before calling the relevant action tool. (Conversation 0: assistant asked "What backlight would you like?" and asked thermostat color.)
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): IF the assistant earlier asked a clarifying question and the user replies affirmatively, the assistant may ask a final "is there nothing else to do?" style prompt before proceeding. (Conversation 15.)

5) Tool Usage Constraints
- IF the assistant will call find_user_id_by_name_zip → THEN the precondition is that the user provided first_name, last_name, and zip; otherwise the call is not attempted. (Conversations 0, 113, 15.)
- IF the assistant will call find_user_id_by_email → THEN the precondition is that the user provided an email; otherwise the call is not attempted. (Conversation 11.)
- IF the assistant will call get_user_details → THEN the precondition is a user_id returned by a find_user_id_by_* call. (All conversations.)
- IF the assistant will call get_order_details → THEN the precondition is an order_id obtained from get_user_details.orders (the assistant iterates over those order IDs). (All conversations.)
- IF the assistant will call get_product_details → THEN the precondition is a product_id for the product the user wants to change or inspect. (Conversations 0, 15.)
- IF the assistant will call list_all_product_types → THEN there is no argument required; the assistant used it to map a product type name to a product_id before calling get_product_details. Observed once. (Conversation 15.) — Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory).
- IF the assistant will call exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, or modify_pending_order_items → THEN the preconditions are:
  - the specified order.status must be "delivered" for exchange/return, and "pending" for modify;
  - the order must contain the item_ids being replaced (verified via get_order_details);
  - a valid payment_method_id for the order must be available (taken from payment_history). (Conversations 0, 11, 15.)
- IF the assistant will call cancel_pending_order → THEN the precondition is the order.status == "pending" and the assistant has a cancellation reason from the user. (Conversation 113.)
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): When an attempted tool name failed (ind_user_id_by_email), the assistant retried with a different tool name. This occurred once. (Conversation 11.)

6) State-Dependent Rules
- IF order.status == "pending" → THEN the assistant uses modify_pending_order_items to change item variants or cancel_pending_order to cancel the order; the assistant does not call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items on pending orders in the provided traces. (Conversations 113, 15.)
- IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN the assistant uses return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items to request returns or exchanges; the assistant does not use modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order on delivered orders in the provided traces. (Conversations 0, 11.)
- IF a user has multiple payment methods across orders and asks for refunds to a different payment method than the order’s original → THEN the assistant informs the user that refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card (if present) and requests an alternative; if the user insists on proceeding the assistant uses the original payment method. (Conversation 11.)
- IF a variant the user requests is available but has a different price → THEN the exchange/modify tool is called with the selected variant and the resulting exchange_price_difference may be negative or positive as returned by the tool (example: exchange_price_difference returned -16.63). The assistant executes the tool regardless and reports the change (Conversation 0). (Observed result; no evidence that price difference blocks the action.)

Notes on dependencies and sequencing (explicit)
- Sequence: [User supplies identification] → [find_user_id_by_*] → [get_user_details] → [for each order_id in user.orders: get_order_details(order_id)] → [if product option changes needed: list_all_product_types (sometimes) → get_product_details(product_id)] → [clarify missing variant attributes] → [ask user confirmation] → [call appropriate order-modifying tool with order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids (if applicable), payment_method_id or reason].
- This exact sequence was followed in all examples where a change was performed. (All four conversations.)

End of extracted rule set.
